title: windows.debugregisters
description:
Gathers information related to the debug registers for the given thread
        Args:
            ethread: the thread (_ETHREAD) to examine
        Returns:
            Tuple[interfaces.objects.ObjectInterface, int, int, int, int, int]: The owner process of the thread and the values for dr7, dr0, dr1, dr2, dr3

title: windows.deskscan
description: Scans for the Desktop instances of each Window Station

description: Enumerates the Desktop instances of each Window Station

description: Listing tree based on drivers and attached devices in a particular windows memory image.

description: Detects the Direct System Call technique used to bypass EDRs (deprecated).

title: windows.drivermodule
description: Determines if any loaded drivers were hidden by a rootkit (deprecated).

title: windows.driverscan
description: Scans for drivers present in a particular windows memory image.

title: windows.dumpfiles
description: Dumps cached file contents from Windows memory samples.

title: windows.envars
description:
Enumerate persistent & common variables.

        This function collects the global (all users) and
        user-specific environment variables from the
        registry. Any variables in a process env block that
        does not exist in the persistent list was explicitly
        set with the SetEnvironmentVariable() API.

title: windows.etwpatch
description:
Identifies ETW (Event Tracing for Windows) patching techniques used by malware to evade detection.

    This plugin examines the first opcode of key ETW functions in ntdll.dll and advapi32.dll
    to detect common ETW bypass techniques such as return pointer manipulation (RET) or function
    redirection (JMP). Attackers often patch these functions to prevent security tools from
    receiving telemetry about process execution, API calls, and other system events.

description: Scans for file objects present in a particular windows memory image.

Interface for PE symbol finding classes

Class to maintain the different possible PoolTypes The values must be

description: Scans for processes present in a particular windows memory image.

title: windows.pstree
description: Plugin for listing processes in a tree based on their parent process ID.

title: windows.psxview
description:
Lists all processes found via four of the methods described in \"The Art of Memory Forensics\" which may help \
    identify processes that are trying to hide themselves.

    We recommend using -r pretty if you are looking at this plugin's output in a terminal.
    deprecated.

title: windows.scheduled_tasks
description:
Decodes scheduled task information from the Windows registry, including
    information about triggers, actions, run times, and creation times (deprecated).

description: lists Processes with Session information extracted from Environmental Variables

    to customize the output.

title: Volatility3 Linux 教程
description: Volatility3 linux 内存取证教程
tips: You can also filter and inspect available plugins using more sophisticated patterns or tools like ``grep``, ``awk``, or simply explore the source under ``volatility3/framework/plugins/linux``.

title: Volatility3 Mac 教程
description: Volatility3 mac 内存取证教程
tips: Here the command is piped to grep and head to provide the start of the list of macOS plugins.

title: Volatility3 Windows 教程
description: Volatility3 windows 内存取证教程
commands: python3 vol.py -f <path to memory image> plugin_name plugin_option
tips: Here the command is piped to grep and head to provide the start of a list of the available windows plugins.
Here the command is piped to head to provide smaller output, here listing only the first 20.
